items.original_candidate: engaging in the war / the imperialist war
items.new_candidate: engin in their war [unverified — likely 'engaging in their war' or 'the imperialist war'; see evidence]
items.evidence: Context: Howard is describing his September 1969 trip to Japan with Roberta Alexander, speaking at a rally organized by Japanese student groups (Beheiren / Zenkyōtō / Zengakuren) at the height of Japan's anti-Vietnam-War movement. The corrected SRT (lines 1977-1979, cue 495) renders a parallel formulation as 'anti-war rally, railing against the enemy's war' — distinct from but topically aligned with the disputed phrase. External sources (MuckRock FBI files article, KQED obituary, Online Archive of California Howard papers finding aid, Wikipedia's '1968-1969 Japanese university protests' and 'Zengakuren' entries) confirm the Vietnam-War subject matter and the Panthers' standard 'anti-imperialist' framing of that war (Cleaver was the chief architect of this discourse per BPP scholarship). However, none of the external sources reproduce the exact wording of Howard's specific utterance. The phonetic mapping 'engin in their war' is far closer to 'engaging in their war' / 'engaging in the war' than to 'the imperialist war' (4-5 syllables vs. 4-5 phonemes is a stretch). The catalog K-ext entry codifying 'the imperialist war' represents the Panthers' rhetorical register correctly but cannot be confirmed for this specific sentence without either an authoritative LoC-published transcript (loc.gov/item/2016655436 returns 403) or an audio recheck. Smithsonian-grade discipline: prefer a flagged literal Whisper rendering over a confident-sounding fabricated canonical choice. Recommend audio recheck or LoC transcript consultation to resolve.
